_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Fuel cost monitoring and carrier rate management systems:**
The workspace must ensure its transport cost management systems capture the current Estonian diesel excise rate of 428 euros per 1,000 litres and flag any legislative change. The government's cancellation of the May 1, 2026 increases (857 SE) means rates are held at pre-increase levels; however, the Ministry of Finance has signalled a materially larger jump in May 2027. Road freight pricing models for Estonia trade lanes — particularly for cargo verticals such as live events equipment, high-value automotive, and humanitarian relief routed via Tallinn — must be updated to reflect this deferred escalation risk. Gap from baseline: most standard TMS/rate-card templates do not carry multi-year excise risk flags; a manual annotation or custom field is needed.

**VAT rate scenario tracking:**
If the Centre Party's motor fuel VAT bill progresses beyond first reading, the workspace's accounts payable and input VAT recovery workflows in Estonia will require reconfiguration. The standard 24% VAT on fuel would be replaced by a 13% rate for one year, affecting the gross fuel cost on supplier invoices and the quantum of reclaimable input VAT. The workspace's finance team should prepare a scenario model now rather than waiting for enactment. Scope: applies to all fuel purchases by the workspace or its carriers on Estonian territory. Deadline: model to be ready before any second reading vote (timing uncertain; monitor Riigikogu calendar).

**Baltic routing competitive analysis:**
Operationally, the workspace should build or update a routing decision tool that compares diesel excise effective rates across Estonia (428 €/1,000 L), Latvia (396 €/1,000 L temporarily through June 30, 2026), Lithuania (553.6 €/1,000 L), and Poland (EU minimum). For road-primary cargo on the Americas/Europe/Asia trade lanes transiting the Baltic corridor, even modest per-litre differentials aggregate meaningfully across multi-tonne high-value shipments. Gap from baseline: this comparative rate table is not a standard feature of freight procurement systems and typically requires manual maintenance.

**Supplier and carrier onboarding — excise compliance confirmation:**
The workspace should add a standard due-diligence question to its Estonian carrier onboarding checklist: confirmation of EMTA licensing status (excise warehouse keeper, registered consignee, or certified consignor as applicable). This is relevant where the workspace subcontracts carriers who handle bonded fuel or cross-border fuel movements for events or production logistics. No dedicated training module is triggered by the May 11 session items, but awareness of the excise suspension framework under EMCS/SADHES should be part of the compliance briefing for operations staff handling Estonia-routed shipments.

*Source: Estonian Tax and Customs Board — Fuel and Electricity, EMTA. https://www.emta.ee/en/business-client/taxes-and-payment/excise-duties/fuel-and-electricity. ERR News, "Business groups seek EU minimum for diesel excise in Estonia," April 8, 2026. https://news.err.ee/1609989957/business-groups-seek-eu-minimum-for-diesel-excise-in-estonia.*
